title: The Butlers Son Did It

jokes.text: See, now this is called the-severed-finger-in-a-box trick. See, you just slide your finger up in the bottom and then voilà.
jokes.setup: Will demonstrates a magic trick with elaborate explanation
jokes.punchline: The trick is revealed to be simple finger placement in a box
jokes.explanation: Basic magic trick humor, fits Will's playful character but nothing exceptional

jokes.text: Ha, ha, ha. Ow!
jokes.setup: Carlton falls for Will's prank with the finger box
jokes.punchline: His scream of pain after being fooled
jokes.explanation: Classic prank payoff, good character moment for Carlton being gullible

jokes.text: I don't know. I'll give it a try... but I've been trying to get G for years, he don't fall for nothing.
jokes.setup: Will wants to prank Geoffrey next
jokes.punchline: Admits Geoffrey is too smart to fall for pranks
jokes.explanation: Sets up Geoffrey's intelligence while building tension for the prank

jokes.text: I'm looking for Geoffrey. - I'm Geoffrey. - Then you must be my father.
jokes.setup: Young man looking for Geoffrey at the door
jokes.punchline: Immediately claims Geoffrey as his father based on name alone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - audience thinks it's another prank but it's the episode's main plot twist

jokes.text: Well, he fell for that.
jokes.setup: Will thinks his prank worked on Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstands the serious father-son revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Will's obliviousness to the gravity of the situation

jokes.text: Where'd you get that outstanding cashmere jacket?
jokes.setup: Philip interrogating the young stranger
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by admiring the boy's expensive clothing
jokes.explanation: Classic Uncle Phil materialistic moment, good character consistency

jokes.text: There's a church next door. If you sit through a sermon you can eat for free. Bye-bye. Ta-ta. Cheerio.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey trying to get rid of his alleged son
jokes.punchline: Offers helpful homeless advice in his polite British manner
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry British humor while trying to politely dismiss the situation

jokes.text: Barbara?
jokes.setup: Frederick reveals Geoffrey's middle name is Barbara
jokes.punchline: Philip's shocked reaction to this feminine middle name
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction humor to unexpected information

jokes.text: It's a family name.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey embarrassed about his feminine middle name
jokes.punchline: Tries to dignify it with deadpan explanation
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dignified response to embarrassing revelation stays true to character

jokes.text: How can Geoffrey be your dad if he was never married?
jokes.setup: Child asking innocent logical question
jokes.punchline: Points out the obvious implication about unmarried parenthood
jokes.explanation: Child's innocent question creates adult awkwardness, classic sitcom device

jokes.text: Well, you see, Nicky, sometimes the stork's arms get real tired, right? He pretty much leave the baby wherever he at.
jokes.setup: Will attempting to explain unmarried parenthood to a child
jokes.punchline: Creates ridiculous stork story about tired arms and random baby dropping
jokes.explanation: Will's street-smart approach to explaining adult topics to kids in his unique voice

jokes.text: Well, Nicky, sometimes the stork's map gets all scrunched up.
jokes.setup: Philip trying his own version of the stork explanation
jokes.punchline: Continues Will's absurd stork metaphor with map navigation problems
jokes.explanation: Philip building on Will's ridiculous explanation, showing family unity in awkwardness

jokes.text: That's right, G. Stick to your story, man. At least till the blood test come out, man.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey denying he has children
jokes.punchline: Will treating it like a paternity scandal from daytime TV
jokes.explanation: Will's pop culture reference approach to serious family drama

jokes.text: Yo, he got the same interests as Miss July.
jokes.setup: Frederick lists his refined British interests
jokes.punchline: Will compares them to a Playboy centerfold's listed hobbies
jokes.explanation: Sharp pop culture observation about generic hobby lists, very Will-like perspective

jokes.text: That is, my room and half a shelf in the refrigerator.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey generously offering his house to his son
jokes.punchline: Reveals the modest reality of a butler's accommodations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - generous gesture undercut by humble reality

jokes.text: We'll stay up late, play Clue and try on each other's jackets. Heh, heh, heh.
jokes.setup: Carlton suggesting fun activities with Frederick
jokes.punchline: Reveals his idea of fun is incredibly dorky and potentially homoerotic
jokes.explanation: Peak Carlton - innocent but awkward suggestions that reveal his sheltered nature

jokes.text: Do I?
jokes.setup: Frederick asks if Carlton has Tom Jones records
jokes.punchline: Carlton's enthusiastic response implies he's a huge Tom Jones fan
jokes.explanation: Carlton's predictable enthusiasm for uncool music, good character consistency

jokes.text: I am. This is fascinating. - What's it about? - Me.
jokes.setup: Hilary appears to be reading a book
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's about herself, showing her narcissism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - her self-obsession presented as intellectual curiosity

jokes.text: Kind of a riches-to-riches story, huh?
jokes.setup: Hilary wants to write about her life story
jokes.punchline: Philip's play on 'rags-to-riches' acknowledging she was always wealthy
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Hilary's privileged life story

jokes.text: Who is this poor little homely child next to me? Uh, sweetheart, that was you.
jokes.setup: Hilary commenting on an ugly child in her kindergarten photo
jokes.punchline: Philip reveals she's looking at herself, not someone else
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with good setup and satisfying reveal about vanity

jokes.text: You mean, you lied to me? You told me I was pretty when I wasn't? - Well... - Thank you. You really do love me.
jokes.setup: Hilary discovering her parents lied about her childhood appearance
jokes.punchline: Instead of being upset, she's touched by their loving deception
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation of anger into gratitude, good character development moment

jokes.text: Geoffrey, I hate to break this to you, but you're about 10 years too late. Boys Frederick's age don't play with toys.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey buying toys for his grown son
jokes.punchline: Philip pointing out the obvious age inappropriateness
jokes.explanation: Sets up the next joke but fairly predictable observation

jokes.text: Transformers. I've been looking for Razorbeast since, like, October.
jokes.setup: Philip just said boys Frederick's age don't play with toys
jokes.punchline: Frederick immediately contradicts this by getting excited about Transformers
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction of Philip's assumption, good timing

jokes.text: Bow before Razorbeast or I will crush you.
jokes.setup: Will playing with the Transformer toy
jokes.punchline: Gets completely absorbed in dramatic toy battle narration
jokes.explanation: Will's childlike enthusiasm showing his fun side, decent character moment

jokes.text: I don't even know whether he would prefer a loose or a firm pleat.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey worrying about not knowing his son
jokes.punchline: Reveals his concerns are about very specific butler-related preferences
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Geoffrey's butler mindset applied to parenting anxieties

jokes.text: Then he doesn't have to know his dad throws like a girl.
jokes.setup: Philip reassuring Geoffrey he doesn't have to play catch
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey admits his athletic inadequacy with self-deprecating humor
jokes.explanation: Shows Geoffrey's vulnerability and self-awareness about his limitations

jokes.text: Have you noticed how Father holds that dish perfectly balanced in his right hand... with his fingers just barely steadying it? Superb, Father.
jokes.setup: Family dinner with Frederick as guest
jokes.punchline: Frederick obsessively praising Geoffrey's basic serving technique
jokes.explanation: Awkward over-enthusiasm creates cringe humor, shows Frederick trying too hard

jokes.text: England must be really boring.
jokes.setup: Frederick's excessive fascination with basic serving techniques
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt assessment of what this implies about England
jokes.explanation: Will's direct, culturally insensitive but funny observation cuts through the pretense

jokes.text: None taken, Miss Hilary. I always consider the source.
jokes.setup: Hilary makes insulting comment about Geoffrey's work
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's politely devastating comeback about her intelligence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - elegant insult delivered with butler politeness

jokes.text: Ah, to be a CAB grad and wear the school colors, black and white.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey mentions the Cambridge Academy of Butlering
jokes.punchline: School colors are black and white - butler uniform colors
jokes.explanation: Subtle joke about butler training school having uniform colors as school colors

jokes.text: No. CAB is very expensive. No, it's a dream that shall never be.
jokes.setup: Frederick expressing desire to attend Cambridge Academy of Butlering
jokes.punchline: Dramatically declares it an impossible dream due to cost
jokes.explanation: Sets up the scam but fairly obvious manipulation, not particularly clever

jokes.text: I wanna be a butler too. No, you don't.
jokes.setup: Nicky inspired by butler conversation
jokes.punchline: Philip's immediate shut-down of his son's career aspiration
jokes.explanation: Philip's class consciousness showing - good character moment

jokes.text: Man, the way this guy talk cracks me up. What y'all people call this? A clock. "A clock." Heh, heh, heh. I gotta remember that.
jokes.setup: Jazz fascinated by Frederick's British accent
jokes.punchline: Treats 'clock' as exotic foreign vocabulary worth remembering
jokes.explanation: Jazz's amusing misunderstanding of accents vs. different vocabulary

jokes.text: What y'all people call this? A clock. 'A clock.' Heh, heh, heh. I gotta remember that.
jokes.setup: Jazz testing if British people have different words for everything
jokes.punchline: Gets excited about 'remembering' that clocks are called clocks
jokes.explanation: Jazz's confusion about British terminology creates absurd moment

jokes.text: All right. Excuse me one second, bro. I gotta go check my super secret stash.
jokes.setup: No one has money for pizza delivery
jokes.punchline: Will reveals he has a hidden money stash with dramatic secrecy
jokes.explanation: Sets up the theft plot while showing Will's secretiveness about money

jokes.text: You ain't special. Fall in line, Brit boy.
jokes.setup: Will making everyone face the wall while he accesses his stash
jokes.punchline: Treats Frederick like a criminal in a police lineup
jokes.explanation: Will's paranoid security measures create humor through overreaction

jokes.text: "The bathroom." Damn, I gotta learn how to speak that language.
jokes.setup: Frederick uses formal term 'bathroom'
jokes.punchline: Jazz thinks British English is a completely different language
jokes.explanation: Continues Jazz's misunderstanding of British accent as foreign language

jokes.text: Give me my money, man. I don't know what you're talking about.
jokes.setup: Will catches Frederick stealing from his secret stash
jokes.punchline: Frederick's unconvincing denial while clearly caught red-handed
jokes.explanation: Standard confrontation setup, nothing particularly clever about the exchange

jokes.text: Oh, no. I'm about to beat you down. Let's go, boy. Come on. Come on.
jokes.setup: Will threatening to fight Frederick
jokes.punchline: His street tough persona contrasted with silly 'beat you down' phrasing
jokes.explanation: Will's tough guy act played for laughs but not particularly original

jokes.text: Boy, let me tell you. You are so lucky that you Geoffrey's son. - I'm glad it finally comes in handy for something.
jokes.setup: Will saying Frederick is lucky to be Geoffrey's son
jokes.punchline: Frederick's sarcastic response about his parentage finally being useful
jokes.explanation: Shows Frederick's bitter attitude toward his situation with Geoffrey

jokes.text: Oh, please. Me be a butler, serving the likes of you?
jokes.setup: Will saying he wanted to be like Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Frederick's disdainful response reveals his true feelings about service
jokes.explanation: Reveals Frederick's class consciousness and deception about wanting to be a butler

jokes.text: Oh, no. I haven't grown into my nose.
jokes.setup: Will asking hypothetically about hurtful truths
jokes.punchline: Hilary immediately assumes it's about her childhood appearance
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier nose joke while showing Hilary's self-obsession

jokes.text: You look like an ass.
jokes.setup: Carlton wearing British bowler hat and umbrella
jokes.punchline: Frederick's blunt assessment of Carlton's costume attempt
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective put-down of Carlton's ridiculous British costume

jokes.text: I'll have you know this is a bona fide British bowler and bumbershoot. I don't know which is which, but I paid good money for the set.
jokes.setup: Carlton defending his British accessories
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't know which item is which
jokes.explanation: Carlton's earnest attempt undermined by his own admission of ignorance

jokes.text: I cannot believe you sweating this fool.
jokes.setup: Will confronting Frederick about the theft
jokes.punchline: Carlton defending Frederick by calling Will the fool
jokes.explanation: Shows Carlton's misguided loyalty and rivalry with Will

jokes.text: Carlton, did you hear what I said? You just can't get over the fact that there's a couple new guns in town.
jokes.setup: Will trying to explain Frederick's theft
jokes.punchline: Carlton dismisses it as jealousy about competition with women
jokes.explanation: Carlton's misreading of the situation while trying to sound cool

jokes.text: If you don't stop talking like that it's gonna be in your leg.
jokes.setup: Frederick continuing to speak in exaggerated British slang
jokes.punchline: Will threatens violence with a dinner fork over annoying accent
jokes.explanation: Will's frustration boiling over into specific, absurd threat

jokes.text: Ow!
jokes.setup: Geoffrey giving Frederick an enthusiastic hug
jokes.punchline: Frederick's pained reaction to the emotional embrace
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy reaction, nothing particularly clever

jokes.text: Oh, who scraped the icing off your cupcake?
jokes.setup: Will looking angry about Frederick
jokes.punchline: Carlton's cutesy way of asking what's wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Carlton phrase - childish and sheltered way of addressing anger

jokes.text: Oh, who scraped the icing off your cupcake?
jokes.setup: Will looking upset
jokes.punchline: Carlton's characteristically dorky way of asking what's wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton phrase - overly cutesy metaphor for being upset

jokes.text: I think he went to go get his suitcase repaired.
jokes.setup: Frederick has disappeared with the money
jokes.punchline: Carlton's naive explanation for why someone would leave with luggage
jokes.explanation: Shows Carlton's innocent inability to recognize obvious deception

jokes.text: Hey, my bowler's gone. That limey thief.
jokes.setup: Carlton finally realizes Frederick has stolen from him too
jokes.punchline: His delayed recognition and use of ethnic slur 'limey'
jokes.explanation: Carlton's slow realization and shift from defending to condemning Frederick

jokes.text: What, were you just sitting around one day sipping tea and crumpets and just decided: 'Hey, I'm gonna fly halfway around the Earth... to go steal from the father that I don't even know'?
jokes.setup: Will confronting Frederick at the airport
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous scenario of casual decision to commit international fraud
jokes.explanation: Will's dramatic confrontation with absurd British stereotype imagery

jokes.text: Yeah, he'll pretty much kick you.
jokes.setup: Will trying to praise Geoffrey's loyalty when you're down
jokes.punchline: Accidentally describes Geoffrey as someone who kicks people when they're down
jokes.explanation: Will's attempt at heartfelt speech goes wrong, creating honest but funny description

jokes.text: No, no, I'm saying though, it's like you come home late, you know, to the crib... you don't want your parents to know, Geoffrey... will dime you out.
jokes.setup: Will trying again to praise Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Again accidentally describes Geoffrey's negative trait of tattling
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern of Will's failed attempt to praise Geoffrey

jokes.text: All right? Because I'm about to practically annihilate you. All right? But I'm gonna get right to it. All right? Because this is gonna sting real, real bad. I ain't doing no pussy-footing around...
jokes.setup: Will trying to deliver bad news to Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Gets caught in a loop of warning about how bad the news will be without delivering it
jokes.explanation: Will's avoidance and over-dramatic buildup creates tension and humor

jokes.text: Master William... hurt me already.
jokes.setup: Will's endless buildup to delivering bad news
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's exasperated request to just get it over with
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey response - polite but exasperated, cuts through Will's stalling

jokes.text: Three out of four ain't bad.
jokes.setup: Will's predictions about Frederick were mostly right but Frederick returned
jokes.punchline: Will's casual assessment of his detective accuracy
jokes.explanation: Will's nonchalant response to being mostly right about a serious situation

jokes.text: Will, I thought you said we was going out tonight? Here I come, y'all.
jokes.setup: Serious emotional moment between Geoffrey and Frederick
jokes.punchline: Jazz's completely oblivious interruption about social plans
jokes.explanation: Jazz's perfect timing for inappropriate interruption provides comic relief

tmdb_overview: Geoffrey's life changes overnight when a man claiming to be his long-lost son shows up and charms everyone - except Will, who discovers the guy's not what he seems. Meanwhile, Hilary starts writing her autobiography, and an early childhood photo reveals a shocking secret.
